seed_problem: Themed grid network; remove at most k links to maximize the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores achieved delay.
mutation: output: interdict VERTICES instead of edges -- collapse at most k chambers (with s,t protected), which seals all incident passages, to maximize the s-t shortest path; the grid is augmented with heavy-tailed long-range crawlways so the vertex shortest-path interdiction is NP-hard and myopic-vs-search heuristics diverge.
title: Cave-In: Collapsing Chambers to Strand the Rival Expedition
strategies: do-nothing baseline: collapse no chambers (rival keeps the original fastest route)
greedy proxy heuristic: repeatedly collapse the current-route intermediate chamber with the largest incident passage weight, checking connectivity only
best-improvement local search: at each step evaluate the true post-collapse shortest route for every collapsible chamber on the current route and commit the genuine best
randomized multi-restart: perturb the best-improvement choices across seeded restarts and keep the collapse set that maximizes the final detour
